key_insight: The study maps 126 five-metal high-entropy boride compositions and shows that synthesizability and mechanical stability are strongly composition-dependent, with Cr-containing systems often performing worse.
engineering_relevance: It provides a screening roadmap for selecting single-phase high-entropy borides with better predicted stability and mechanical performance for ultra-high-temperature structural applications.
method: DFT calculated phase and mechanical behavior for all 126 combinations of nine group 4–6 transition metals in hexagonal AlB2-type HEBs.
Used the entropy forming ability (EFA) descriptor to estimate single-phase synthesizability.
Trained machine learning models on the computed dataset to identify composition-property trends.
evidence_signal: The EFA predictions were reported to agree well with available experimental data for selected high-entropy borides, supporting the screening approach.
watch_item: Predictions rely on idealized DFT/SQS and ML screening, so experimental validation is still needed, especially for metastability and synthesis-kinetics effects.
paper_title: Synthesizability and Mechanical Properties of High-Entropy Borides: First-Principles and Machine Learning Studies
